_comment: Domain-specific extensions not covered by mime-db. Each section documents WHY the extension is here. The `_exclude` key lists extensions to drop from the mime-db-derived set.

_exclude._note: Extensions mime-db marks compressible but we deliberately skip. Reasons: (1) archive-extractor inputs must remain identity-encoded (.tar); (2) already-compressed formats where re-gzip wastes CPU or inflates size (svgz/x3dz pre-gzipped; ova is a tar; psd uses RLE/ZIP internally; dds is DXT/BCn compressed); (3) VM disk images (vdi/vmdk/vhd/hdd) are huge sparse/compressed binaries not meaningful to gzip.

3d_tiles._note: Cesium 3D Tiles formats (not registered with IANA but standard practice to gzip).

cesium_terrain._note: Cesium quantized-mesh terrain tiles. Compress only if served raw (not pre-gzipped by Cesium ion).

json_streams._note: Newline-delimited JSON variants. Not in mime-db.

geospatial_text._note: Text-form geospatial formats not in mime-db.

protobuf_misc._note: Protobuf encodings; application/protobuf in mime-db has no compressible flag set.

mvt._note: application/vnd.mapbox-vector-tile is registered in mime-db but has no compressible flag. Mapbox spec assumes Content-Encoding: gzip.

octet_stream_safe._note: application/octet-stream is excluded wholesale because its extensions list includes executables (exe, dll, dmg, iso). Re-add only the safe ones we want to compress.

tabular._note: TSV is not always in mime-db extensions.
